title: Palantir Just Joined the S&P 500. History Says the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Do This Next.
text: The article discusses Palantir Technologies' recent inclusion in the S&P 500 index and analyzes what this milestone might mean for the company's stock performance. S&P 500 inclusion typically brings visibility and investment from retail investors and index-tracking funds. However, research from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York suggests that any direct effect is short-lived, with pre-existing momentum being the primary driver of post-inclusion performance. Regarding Palantir's fundamentals, the piece notes the company demonstrates strong revenue growth and significant operating expense reductions, resulting in substantial net income increases. The company continues securing government contracts, including a recent deal with the U.S. Army. Despite positive business trends, the author expresses significant reservations about the investment case. Palantir carries a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 103 and price-to-sales ratio of 41, substantially higher than competitors like Nvidia (46 P/E, 26 P/S) and Alphabet (21 P/E, 6 P/S). The author concludes that while Palantir looks promising operationally, its valuation makes it unsuitable for most investors, though it could appeal to younger investors with high risk tolerance and long time horizons.
